selectionPrinciple: Prefer the smallest local/offline pipeline that clears the production gates. Do not switch models until the benchmark proves the current failure is model-limited rather than preprocessing/postprocessing-limited.

candidates.description: Current pinned PP-OCRv5 mobile detector + Korean mobile recognizer.
candidates.whyTest: Provides the baseline. No candidate is meaningful until it beats this on the same golden dataset.

candidates.description: Keep current models but add orientation variants, real DB polygon postprocess, and perspective text-line crops.
candidates.whyTest: Most likely first win: it targets the known failure mode without increasing model size.

candidates.description: Evaluate larger/newer Korean-compatible recognizers only after crop/orientation fixes are measured.
candidates.whyTest: Useful if corrected crops still miss dense Korean receipt text.
expectedStrengths: higher character accuracy on small text

candidates.runtime: on-device OCR adapters behind the existing recognizer contract
candidates.description: Run two local OCR candidates or preprocessing variants and choose field candidates by evidence score.
candidates.whyTest: Useful when no single preprocessing/model path dominates all receipt layouts.

candidates.runtime: same local OCR model, repeated on preview frames
candidates.description: Instead of one still photo, sample frames every ~500 ms during capture and fill fields when repeated high-confidence text stabilizes.
candidates.whyTest: Can beat still-photo OCR without a bigger model by selecting sharper frames and accumulating evidence.

candidates.description: Escalate a user-approved receipt image or anonymized crop to multimodal reasoning only for review, never default OCR.
candidates.whyTest: Potentially strong for complex forms, but privacy and latency make it a non-default fallback.
